Makes the off-site replica a first-class citizen — browse, restore and maintain it like the local repo, on its own schedule — and adds live progress to the flash download. Thanks to **@manilx** for the steady, detailed feedback that shaped all of this.

## Added
- **Restore from local or off-site.** Every backup browser (Containers, VMs, Flash) and the Integrity & Maintenance card now has a **Local | Off-site** switch. List, restore, download, verify, unlock, prune and delete against whichever copy you pick — so if a local repo is lost or corrupt, you can restore straight from the off-site replica.
- **Off-site replication on its own schedule.** Each domain gets an optional off-site schedule (Settings → Off-site copy). Leave it blank to replicate after every local backup (as before), or set a cadence — e.g. `weekly Sun 03:00` — to replicate only then, so you can back up locally every day but ship off-site once a week. A **Replicate now** button runs it on demand.
- **Live flash-download progress.** The flash `.zip` download now streams with a progress indicator and disables the button while it runs, so it's clear the download has started (no more wondering if a click did anything).

## Changed
- **Delete is per-source.** Deleting a backup only removes the copy you're viewing (local *or* off-site) — deleting a local backup never touches the off-site copy, and vice versa. The browser spells this out next to the source switch.

## Notes
- Off-site schedules accept `off` / `daily HH:MM` / `weekly DOW HH:MM` / cron; `everyN` is rejected for off-site (it has no per-run gate and would fire daily).
- The flash download is buffered in the browser before saving; fine for typical flash images (hundreds of MB).
- Store migrations v16–v18 add the per-domain off-site schedule columns (applied automatically on first start).

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.4.0` (also `:2`, `:2.4`, `:latest`) and `docker.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.4.0`, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
